description: A styled pull-quote blockquote with an optional speaker name and attribution line, used throughout news and content pages.

stories.name: Default
stories.description: Quote with speaker name and attribution

props.quote: More research into the molecular mechanisms by which biosynthetic gene clusters respond to environment stress and shape the soil microbiome could direct future breeding programmes.
props.name: Professor Jurriaan Ton

stories.name: QuoteOnly
stories.description: Quote with no speaker name or attribution

props.quote: There has been a lot of dialogue about the situation, but what we need now is action. Act with hope, act without hope—but act.

stories.name: WithNameNoAttribution
stories.description: Quote with speaker name but no attribution role

props.quote: The tasks were varied and engaging, encouraging us to think about our own practice and experience as educators, and beyond.
props.name: Former DELTA candidate

stories.name: Group
stories.description: Multiple quotes as they might appear on a giving/impact page

instances.quote: It's really given me an insight into industry professionals and their use of Artificial Intelligence.
instances.name: Ahmed Alyafeai

instances.quote: For our part, we know that many older adults view sexual activity, intimacy, and identity as an integral part of their lives.
instances.name: Professor Sharron Hinchliff
instances.attribution: Professor of Psychology and Health at the School of Allied Health Professions, Nursing and Midwifery

notes:
The quote text always lives inside a

within .uosquote-quote. The name and attribution are optional and rendered as

elements with their respective classes. In some CMS-authored samples the name and attribution text is wrapped in tags — this appears to be inconsistent CMS input; the component renders plain text. The .uosquote class appears on blockquote elements both standalone and nested inside .uosblock.grey — the grey styling comes from the parent UosBlock component, not from the blockquote itself.
